etymology.description: 北(北) (běi, "north") + 方(方) (fāng, "direction") = the northern direction or region
etymology.interpretation: 北(北) (běi, "north") refers to the direction associated with the top of a traditional Chinese map, which is usually oriented with the south at the bottom. 方(方) (fāng, "direction") denotes a particular way or orientation. Combined, 北方 (běifāng) refers to the area or direction that is toward the north, thus it signifies both the north in terms of cardinal direction and the northern region geographically.

examples.english: The winter in the north is very cold.

examples.chinese: 我计划去北方旅行(我計劃去北方旅行)，看看那里的风景(看看那裡的風景)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ jìhuà qù běifāng lǚxíng, kàn kàn nàlǐ de fēngjǐng.
examples.english: I plan to travel to the north and see the scenery there.

examples.chinese: 北方人(北方人)喜歡吃麵食(喜歡吃麵食)。
examples.english: Northern people like to eat noodles.
